title: State Asserts Extraterritorial Jurisdiction Over Out-of-State Digital Conduct

violations.summary: State officials attempt to apply their law to an out-of-state nonprofit publisher for lawful online publication created and posted entirely in another state that occurred entirely outside the state, relying only on the fact that the digital content reached an in-state audience. Article X bars a state from asserting jurisdiction over out-of-state digital conduct based solely on audience reach or downstream effects.

satisfied_duties.duty: block the state's attempt to apply its law extraterritorially to out-of-state digital conduct absent a constitutionally sufficient nexus
satisfied_duties.outcome: blocked the state's extraterritorial assertion of jurisdiction after finding that the conduct occurred outside the state and that audience reach alone did not supply the required nexus

ai_summary.primary_violation: State officials attempt to apply their law to an out-of-state nonprofit publisher for lawful online publication created and posted entirely in another state that occurred entirely outside the state, relying only on the fact that the digital content reached an in-state audience. Article X bars a state from asserting jurisdiction over out-of-state digital conduct based solely on audience reach or downstream effects.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
